examples.title: Constructor Overloading Example - Complex Number Class
examples.description: Demonstrates default, parameterized, and copy constructors
examples.explanation: Three constructors demonstrate overloading. Each constructor has distinct parameter list. Compiler automatically selects based on how object is created.

examples.title: Deep Copy Constructor Implementation
examples.description: Shows proper deep copy for class with pointer member
examples.explanation: Deep copy constructor allocates new memory before copying data. This prevents both objects from pointing to same memory, avoiding double-free on destruction.

examples.title: Constructor Delegation (C++11)
examples.description: Demonstrates constructor delegation to reduce code duplication
examples.explanation: Constructor delegation (C++11 feature) allows one constructor to call another, avoiding code duplication. The delegated-to constructor completes first, then control returns.

examples.title: Initialization List Demonstration
examples.description: Shows proper use of initialization list with const and reference members
examples.explanation: const members and references cannot be assigned after initialization. Initialization list is mandatory. Members are initialized in declaration order, not list order.

examples.title: Private Constructor - Singleton Pattern
examples.description: Demonstrates private constructor for singleton pattern
examples.explanation: Private constructor prevents direct object creation from outside. Only static method can create instance. This implements Singleton pattern ensuring one instance.
